titleStmt.title: London, British Library, BL Oriental 639
Hymns to Mary, Taʾammǝra Māryām, Māḫleta ṣǝge

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation by the library in the upper right corner. One unnumbered blank leaf at the beginning, referred to as in the library's digitization, and two at the end, referred to as .

binding.decoNote: The manuscript seems to have been rebound by the library.

history.provenance: The manuscript had very probably belonged to a church in the Gondar area, from where it was taken by Tewodros and brought to Madḫāne ʿĀlam. It was then looted by the British Napier expedition in 1868.

bibl.title: Catalogue of the Ethiopic Manuscripts in the British Museum Acquired since the Year 1847

msItem.title: Ṣalot za-ʾǝgzǝʾtǝna Māryām ba-Dabra Golgotā
ʾO-rǝḫrǝḫta ḫǝllinā
Synaxarion commemoration of Garimā

layout.ab: Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

handNote.desc: The hand of and of might be identical.

item.desc: The name of ʾAdarā Giyorgis is mentioned in the supplications of and . Though William Wright states that this is the writer, this is not indicated in the manuscript.

msItem.title: Sǝnkǝssār Commemoration of Mary for the 16th Yakkātit
Yǝweddǝsǝwwa malāʾǝkt la-Māryām
Malkǝʾ-hymn to Our Lady Mary
Hymn to Our Lady Mary
Malkǝʾ-hymn to Our Lady Mary
Hymn to Our Lady Mary
ʾƎsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki wa-ʾǝweddǝsaki
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection: Title
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
Soba ʾaṣḥafa Daqsǝyos taʾammǝrǝki qǝddusa

layout.ab: Primary pricks are visible, horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

decoNote.desc: How the angels show Mary the fire of hell.
How Mary suffered and prayed after being shown hell.

desc.ref: Miracle of Mary: Mary speaks to a worker
Miracle of Mary: The Jew of ʾAkmim
Miracle of Mary: Dǝmyanos who wrote Mary's name in golden ink
Miracle of Mary: ʾAbbās, bishop of Rome
Miracle of Mary: The monk, Yǝsḥaq, who prayed for seven years that St Mary would appear to him

decoNote.desc: Unfinished outline of a miniature of the Miracle of Mary: Mary carries a sick man to Jerusalem

desc.ref: Miracle of Mary: Māryām of Dafrā
Miracle of Mary: The French artist who fell from the scaffolding while painting frescoes
Miracle of Mary: Mary carries a sick man to Jerusalem
Miracle of Mary: Zakāryās, who used to bring roses to the icon of St Mary
Miracle of Mary: Yolyānā and Barbārā from Bethlehem
Miracle of Mary: The three Arabs journeying to Rif and Salomon, the abbot of Qalamon
Miracle of Mary: The church of ʿƎqonā, which was removed to the edge of the sea
Miracle of Mary: The blind priest, Yoḥannǝs Bakansi
Miracle of Mary: The rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow
Miracle of Mary: Elizabeth, the blind daughter of Abraham and Gerā ʾAnǝśt of Badǝrmān
Miracle of Mary: The widow who could not find husbands for her three daughters
Miracle of Mary: The two brothers who copied the Miracles of Mary
Miracle of Mary: Giyorgis Ḥaddis, who was delivered from prison

decoNote.desc: Miracle of Mary: Kaṭir, the old priest at ʾƎlkǝsus. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The two brothers Tāg and Nazib from Dalgā who were dyers. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The stone-footed man from France. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: Bishop Mercurius, whom the patriarch wanted to remove because of leprosy. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The woman with a broken foot from Hartarom. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel, who became pregnant. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: Bārok of Finǝqi whom his enemies could not kill until he had confessed himself to a priest. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The deacon ʾAnǝsṭāsyos who recited the prayer tafaśśǝḥi and was healed by Mary. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The monk of Dabra Qalǝmon who did not fast. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The cannibal of Qǝmǝr. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The son of a widow, who became a thief, is saved by Mary. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: The pregnant woman who was caught by the tide while going to the church of St Michael. Legends and captions are missing.
Miracle of Mary: Mary gives a thirsty dog water to drink from her shoe. Legends and captions are missing.

desc.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the virgin whom a magistrate wanted to corrupt

item.desc: The note states that the manuscript belongs to the church of Madḫāne ʿĀlam
The names of the first owners have been everywhere erased; they were apparently Rǝḫruḫa Krǝstos and Ḥawāryāt.
The names of the later owners were Mikāʾel and Fāsikāwit.

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Described manuscript with the catalogue entry and consulting the digitization.
Updated msParts, added collation and layout
